title: Lemonade introduces autonomous car insurance in Tesla collaboration
text: Lemonade announced launch of Lemonade Autonomous Car insurance, first-of-its-kind product designed for self-driving cars, starting with Tesla FSD. Result of technical collaboration with Tesla, giving Lemonade access to vehicle data previously unavailable. New offering cuts per-mile rates for FSD-engaged driving by approximately 50%, reflecting data showing significantly reduced risk during autonomous operation. Lemonade expects further reductions as Tesla releases FSD software updates, anticipated to make cars even safer. Product rollout begins Arizona on January 26 and Oregon a month later. Stock impact: LMND stock jumped 6% in Wednesday midday trading after unveiling insurance for self-driving cars. Morgan Stanley upgraded Lemonade, Inc. on March 17 to Overweight from Equalweight, increasing price target to $85 from $80. Strategic significance: Partnership aligns Lemonade with autonomous vehicle industry trajectory and Tesla ecosystem. Data access provides competitive advantage in understanding autonomous driving risk profiles.
